texto: High School students in America debate why President Roosevelt didn't bomb the rail lines to Hitler's camps. Their children may ask, a generation from now, why the West stared at far clearer satellite images of Kim Jong Il's camps, and did nothing.
explicacao: Citação de Blaine Harden sobre temas ligados a educacao. “High School students in America debate why President Roosevelt didn't bomb the rail lines to Hitl…” articula educacao, humanity, ignorance em registro de educacao, com relevância para contextos como ignorance e justice.

texto: I am evolving from being an animal,' he said. 'But it is going very, very slowly. Sometime I try to cry and laugh like other people, just to see if it feels like anything. Yet tears don't come. Laughter doesn't come.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Blaine Harden: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I am evolving from being an animal,' he said. 'But it is going very, very slowly. Sometime I try …” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que humor, humanity, recovery pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de recovery.
